series_description:
Incoming/outgoing and internal
leadership, supervisory, managerial,
executive, administrative, substantive,
and other high‐level correspondence in
any media, including electronic
communication, pertaining to the
formulation, planning, implementation,
interpretation, modification, or
redefinition of the programs, services, or
projects of an agency and the
administrative regulations, policies, and
procedures that govern them.
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only the
administrative correspondence of
executive staff, board or
commission members, division
directors and program heads
require archival review. Contact the
State Archives when these records
have met their retention periods.
CAUTION: This records series and
item number 1.1.008 should be
used only for correspondence that
is not included in or directly related
to another records series on the
agency's approved schedule.

series_description:
Non‐administrative incoming/outgoing
and internal correspondence, in any
media, including electronic
communication, pertaining to or arising
from the routine operations of the
policies, programs, services, or projects of
an agency.
comments:
SEE comment to item number
1.1.007. SEE ALSO item number
1.1.010.

series_description:
Calendars, appointment books or
programs and scheduling, or itinerary
records maintained by staff during
business hours that document
appointments, itineraries, and other
activities of agency officials or employees.
retention_statement: Calendar Year End plus 1 year
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only the
calendars, appointment, and
itinerary records of elected
officials, executive staff, board or
commission members, division
directors, and program heads
require archival review. Contact the
State Archives when these records
have met their retention periods.
CAUTION: A record of this type
purchased with personal funds, but
used by a state official or employee
to document his or her work
activities may be a state record and
subject to this retention period.
See Open Records Decision 635
issued in December 1995 by the
Attorney General.

series_description:
Surveys returned by the customers or
clients of an agency, and the statistical
data maintained rating an agency's
performance.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
See item number 1.1.067 for
summary reports compiled from
customer surveys. Includes post‐
audit surveys. SAO policy is to
retain longer than minimum
required by the Texas State Library
and Archives Commission (TSLAC).

series_description:
Notes or text of speeches, papers,
presentations, or reports delivered in
conjunction with agency work.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only speeches,
papers, and presentations given by
or on behalf of executive staff,
board or commission members,
division directors and program
heads require archival review.

series_description:
Records of temporary usefulness that are
not an integral part of a records series of
an agency, that are not regularly filed
within an agency’s recordkeeping system,
and that are required only for a limited
period of time for the completion of an
action by an official or employee of the
agency or in the preparation of an
ongoing records series. Transitory records
are not essential to the fulfillment of
statutory obligations or to the
documentation of agency functions.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Records management
officers should make certain
records are not part of another
records series listed in this
schedule or, for records series
unique to an agency, are not part
of a records series that documents
the fulfillment of the statutory
obligations of the agency or the
documentation of its functions. The
disposal of transitory information
need not be documented through
destruction signoffs (item number
1.2.001) or in records disposition
logs (1.2.010), but agencies should
establish procedures governing
disposal of these records as part of
its records management plan
(1.2.014).

series_title:
Staff Meeting Agendas,
Minutes, and Notes
series_description:
Agendas, minutes, notes, and supporting
documentation, for internal or
interagency meetings.

Annual, sub‐annual, or special reports or
studies on non‐fiscal aspects of an
agency's programs, services, or projects
compiled by agency personnel, by
advisory committees, or by consultants
under contract with an agency that are
not noted elsewhere in this schedule.
Includes reports distributed either
internally or to other entities.
comments:
See item number 1.1.065 for raw
data used to produce reports.
ARCHIVES NOTE: Reports are
archival when they deal with
significant aspects of the agency's
programs.

series_title:
Reports and Studies (Non‐
Fiscal) – Raw Data
series_description:
Information or data collected and
compiled for the purpose of producing
non‐fiscal reports.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable
comments:
See item number 1.1.067 for
reports produced from raw data.
CAUTION: Does not include source
documentation used for
information or data included in or
directly related to another records
series in this schedule.

series_description:
Reports compiled by agency personnel on
a daily or other periodic basis pertaining
to workload monitoring, task completion
times, number of public contacts, and
similar activities.

Master versions of all forms used
internally and externally by the agency,
including subsequent revisions to an
agency form or any associated design or
design modification requests.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
One copy of each state publication as
defined on page xi of the state schedule,
except a publication that is subject to a
different retention period in this schedule.
For example, a meeting agenda (see item
number 1.1.058) also meets the
definition, but it must be retained
permanently; item numbers 1.1.004,
1.1.055, 1.1.066, 1.1.068, and 4.5.003,
which also meet the definition, are closely
associated with the appropriations
process and must be retained AC + 6.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
CAUTION: Many state publications
must be submitted to the Texas
State Publications Depository
Program of TSLAC by law (Texas
Government Code, Sections
441.101‐441.106). TSLAC will retain
a copy of all state publications
submitted to it on a continuing
basis, subject to periodic
evaluation to determine if the
publication merits further
retention. For additional
information and requirements
concerning state publications made
available to the public through the
Internet, consult the administrative
rules of the Texas State
Publications Depository Program of
TSLAC (Title 13, Texas
Administrative Code, Sections 3.1‐
3.16).

series_description:
Physical and digital development and
design files used to create State
Publications, including but not limited to
background material, copy (drafts),
original artwork, photo negatives, prints,
flats, etc. This includes all work performed
both inside and outside the agency.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable
comments:
See item number 1.3.001 for final
State Publications created from
development files. ARCHIVES
NOTE: Successive and substantive
drafts of major publications may be
archival. Major is defined by both
the publication's authorship and its
impact on Texas and Texans.
Original artwork, including photo
prints and negatives that have
significant value as evidence of
agency programs as well as the
potential for re‐use, may be
archival.

series_description:
Documentation used to provide and
improve access to records. Includes
indexes, card files, shelf lists, registers,
guides, and other finding aids.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: These records must
carry the same retention period
and archival code of the records
they support.

series_description:
Work, duty, shift, crew, or case schedules,
rosters, or assignments.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable

series_description:
Reports to the agency from TWC or its
predecessor pertaining to employees.

Any documents detailing the procedural
duties and responsibilities of agency
positions on a position‐by‐position basis.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 3 years

series_description:
Annual Financial Reports (AFR) required
by the General Appropriations Act (100
Day Report).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: The final version
of Annual Financial Reports must
be submitted to the Texas State
Publications Depository Program
per 13 TAC 3.3(a)(2)(a). Working
files and related documentation
used in creating the final plan are
not subject to archival review and
may be disposed of at the
expiration of the retention period.
CAUTION: If an agency does not
produce a biennial or annual
narrative report as described in
item number 1.1.066, then these
annual financial reports must be
marked with Archival Code A. The
archival requirement, when a
biennial or annual narrative report
is not produced, is met by sending
the required copies of the reports
to the Texas State Publications
Depository Program of TSLAC.

series_description:
Includes encumbrances and
documentation about budget transfers
and revisions, as well as detail charts of
accounts.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description: Awards, incentives, tenure, etc.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description:
Plans for evacuation of agency facilities in
case of emergency.
retention_statement: Until Superseded

series_description:
Records of projects conducted by agency
staff that do not fall under another record
series. Includes notes, reference material,
meeting minutes, correspondence, and
supporting documentation related to the
project.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include audit
projects (see agency item number
03.004) or projects relating to
planning new or redefined
programs, services, or projects of
the agency (see agency item
number 01.030).

series_title:
Independence Statements
and Proposed Outside
Employment Requests
series_description:
Forms or other documents used to ensure
auditor independence.

Documentation used to evaluate
employee performance on projects.

Agreements between employee and
agency authorizing certain actions,
including acknowledgement forms,
telecommuting agreements,
outside/secondary employment
authorizations, or other documentation
that show proof of receipt and awareness
of and adherence to agency policies and
procedures.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
Records that document the release of
records or information through any
method other than those listed elsewhere
in this schedule (including employment
verification).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
See agency item number 08.002 for
records released under the Public
Information Act. See item number
1.1.076 for records produced for a
subpoena. See item number
1.1.048 for records produced for
litigation. See agency item number
10.003 for records provided to
members of the Legislature or to
legislative committees. CAUTION:
Some records releases may require
longer retention period. Agencies
must determine if longer retention
period is required based on any
federal or state statutes or
regulations that apply to the
agency's functions. Agency legal
staff should be consulted.

series_title:
Disclosure Statement for
Outside Financial Advisors
and Service Providers
series_description:
Disclosure statements for outside financial
advisors and services providers as
required by Chapter 2263, Texas
Government Code.

Plans and records relating to the process
of planning new or redefined programs,
services, or projects of an agency that are
neither included in nor directly related to
other records series in this schedule.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Data processing
planning records are not archival.

series_description:
Any document that officially initiates,
rescinds, or amends general office
procedures.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 1 year

series_description:
News, press releases, marketing files and
collateral, or any public relations files
maintained or issued by an agency.
Includes print, electronic, audio, and
audiovisual records.

Charts or diagrams that show the
structure of an agency and the
relationships and relative ranks of its
departments and job positions.
retention_statement: Until Superseded
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only charts
showing the overall arrangement
and administrative structure of the
state agency need to be transferred
to the Archives and Information
Services Division, Texas State
Library and Archives Commission.
Organizational charts showing
division/ department level detail
are not considered archival.

series_description:
Official agenda and minutes of the
Legislative Audit Committee
retention_statement: Permanent
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Agency retains
permanent record copy. The
archival requirement will be met by
sending a copy to the Archives and
Information Services Division of
TSLAC. CAUTION: This records
series and item numbers 1.1.060
and 1.1.061 must be used for those
state boards, committees,
commissions, and councils, which
by law or the biennial
Appropriations Act, are
administered by another state
agency. These records and all
others related to the functions of
any of these dependent entities
must be included in the records
retention schedule of the
administering agency.

series_description:
Manuals, guidelines, or similar records
distributed internally for the use of
employees that sets out the policies and
procedures that govern an agency's
programs, services, or projects. Includes
working files and related documentation
used in creating rules, policies, and
procedures.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Working files and
related documentation used in
creating the final rules, policies,
and procedures, are not subject to
archival requirement and may be
disposed of at the expiration of the
retention period.

series_description:
Documentation that helps plan audits,
including templates for future or recurring
audits.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable
comments:
Includes both electronic and paper
documents.

series_description:
Documentation that is kept as transitory
information until all legislative inquiries
are considered complete.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable

series_description:
Classification‐related documentation and
other review documentation.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 10 years

series_description:
Documentation and legal opinions related
to audits and reviews performed by or on
behalf of an agency, including the working
papers that support the audit. Also
includes audits performed on the agency.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 10 years
comments:
The State Auditor's Office retains
any copies of its audits performed
on Texas state agencies. Attest
documentation; Government
Auditing Standards §§ 4.19‐4.24;
6.20‐6.26; 7.77‐7.84 Following
statutory requirement.

retention_statement: Permanent

series_description:
Records documenting the progress of new
auditors.

Including any supporting documentation
created and/or used to justify and support
legislative appropriations requests by an
agency.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: The final version
of Legislative Appropriation
Requests must be submitted to the
Texas State Publications Depository
Program per 13 TAC 3.3(a)(3)(A).
Working files and related
documentation used in creating the
final plan are not subject to
archival review and may be
disposed of at the expiration of the
retention period.

series_description:
Documentation used to start, modify, or
stop all voluntary or required deductions
from payroll, including garnishment or
other court‐ordered attachments.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years
comments:
SAO policy is to retain longer than
minimum required by TSLAC.

series_description:
Payroll records and registers documenting
employee earnings, wages, and pay. This
may include but is not limited to payroll
input records, summary statements,
payroll vouchers, payroll detail sheets,
and payroll history.

Includes 1099, W2, FICA, and other tax
records.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years

series_description:
Used to make increases or decreases to
employees' gross pay, FICA, retirement, or
in the computation of taxes.
comments:
Human Resources keeps this after
termination.

series_description:
Employer's copy of "Employees'
Withholding Exemption Certificate."
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years
comments:
SAO policy is to retain longer than
minimum required by TSLAC.

series_description:
Forms used to deposit employee’s
earnings into a specified personal account.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
Requests and authorizations for travel;
participation in educational programs,
workshops, or college classes; or for other
work‐related activities for which the
expenses of the employee are defrayed or
reimbursed.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Used to create employee leave balances,
to transfer leave balances when an
employee transfers positions, to adjust
carry‐over balances on August 31, to
correct errors on leave accumulation, and
to close out leave accounts on separated
employees.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Cumulative report that is issued each pay
cycle and provides employee leave status
information for each position.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Records created to schedule time worked
by employees outside of or in addition to
their regular working hours, including
approval authorizations.
comments:
CAUTION: Only includes overtime
schedules and authorizations. See
item number 3.4.006 for
timekeeping records.

series_description:
Records documenting individual
employee’s hours worked, including work
schedules and documentation evidencing
adherence to or deviation from normal
hours for those employees working on
fixed schedules.

series_title:
Time Off and/or Sick
Leave Requests
series_description:
Requests and authorizations for vacation,
compensatory, sick, Family and Medical
Leave Act (FMLA), sick pool leave, and
other types of authorized leave, and
supporting documentation.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Requests submitted, approvals, number of
hours transferred in and out, etc.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years
comments:
Keep consistent with ADA
Documentation (1.1.056). SAO
policy is to retain longer than
minimum required by TSLAC.

series_description:
Claims; invoices; statements; copies of
checks and purchase orders; expenditure
authorizations; ledgers; encumbrance,
purchase, general journal, expenditure,
and special vouchers; and similar records
that serve to document disbursements,
including those documenting claims for
and reimbursement to employees for
travel and other expenses.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Bill copies or stubs, statements, billing
registers, account cards, deposit warrants,
cash receipts, credit card receipts, receipts
books, cash transfers, daily cash reports,
reconciliations, general journal vouchers,
special vouchers, and similar records that
serve to document money owed to or
received by a state agency and its
collection or receipt.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Bank statements, credit card statements,
cancelled checks, check registers, deposit
slips, debit and credit notices,
reconciliations, and other banking related
records.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Internal and external periodic fiscal
management reports and associated
worksheets, including federal financial
reports, salary reports, HUB reports,
investment performance reports, etc.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 5 years
comments:
CAUTION: This series does not
include fiscal reports created to
fulfill grant requirements. See item
number 4.7.008.

series_description:
Periodic reports compiled from
information entered into the Uniform
Statewide Accounting System (USAS).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
Yearly report compiled from information
entered into the Uniform Statewide
Accounting System (USAS).
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Records documenting the internal and
external procedural requirements with
respect to the accounting department of a
state agency.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 3 years

series_description:
Records of accounts deemed
uncollectible, including write‐off
authorizations and returned checks,
warrants, and drafts.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years

series_description:
Documentation relating to the capital and
fixed assets of a state agency, including
equipment or property history cards
containing data on initial cost,
depreciation schedules or summaries used
for capital outlay budgeting, and property
sale, auction, or disposal records of
agency owned equipment and property.
retention_statement: Life of Asset plus 3 years

series_description:
Records documenting financial obligations
of a state agency that are not payable
within one year of the date of the balance
sheet, including debentures, loans,
deferred tax liabilities, bonds, and pension
obligations.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years

series_description:
Signature cards or similar records
establishing authority of an agency
employee to initiate or authorize financial
transactions on behalf of an agency.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years

series_description:
Contracts, leases, and agreements include
general obligation, land lease, utilities,
and construction except for buildings.
Documents include specifications,
affidavits of publication of calls for bids,
accepted bids, performance bonds,
contracts, purchase orders, inspection
reports, and correspondence.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years
comments:
See related item number
5.3.007a/b/c for bid
documentation.
See item number 5.2.028 for
building construction contracts.
See item number 5.1.017 for
contract logs.

series_description:
Any mailing address, telephone or fax
number, or e‐mail address records
maintained by an agency on its employees
or on entities or persons it serves.
retention_statement: Until Superseded

series_description:
Records and reports of postage expenses,
including postal meter usage.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_title:
Requisitions for In‐Agency
or Inter‐Agency
Copy/Printing Service
series_description:
Includes word processing and data
processing.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable

series_description:
Licenses and permits obtained from
external agencies or organizations in order
to perform operations.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include
licenses and permits issued by an
agency as part of its statutory
responsibilities.

series_title:
Photocopier and Telefax
Usage Logs and Reports
series_description:
Registers or logs of print copies and fax
transmissions made by user or in total.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable

series_description:
Schedules of prices charged by an agency
for services to the public or other
agencies, including any documentation
used to determine the charges.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 3 years

series_description:
Liability, theft, fire, health, life,
automobile, and other policies for
government property and personnel
including supporting documentation
relevant to the implementation,
modification, renewal, or replacement of
policies.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years

series_description:
Any internally distributed manual,
guidelines, or similar records that
establish standard office procedures for
an agency, e.g. agency style manuals,
telephone protocols, mail room
procedures, and print shop and photocopy
ordering instructions.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 1 year

series_description:
Any record created by an agency to track
any type of incoming and outgoing
correspondence or packages by the U.S.
Postal Service or by private couriers.

List of agency contracts, leases, and
agreements including general obligation,
land lease, utilities, and construction
contracts.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Includes architectural and engineering
drawings, profiles, and blueprints.
retention_statement: Life of Asset
comments:
SEE ALSO item numbers 5.2.002
and 5.2.028. ARCHIVES NOTE:
Archival review designation is for
state‐owned buildings only.

series_description:
Records documenting building space
requests and approvals. Information on
request forms may include but is not
limited to: the building and room; the
reason for the change; parties involved in
the request; date of request and approval.

Records documenting the inventorying,
maintenance, usage, and disposal of
supplies, equipment, and property of a
state agency. Includes lost, stolen, and
damage reports.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years
comments:
See item number 5.2.008 for the
maintenance logs of individual
pieces of equipment.

series_title:
Inspection, Repair, and
Maintenance Records –
Equipment
series_description:
Equipment history file, which may include
logbooks and requests for installation,
moves, service, repair, etc.
retention_statement: Life of Asset plus 3 years
comments:
For service agreements or
contracts related to equipment
repairs and service, retain in
accordance with item number
5.1.001a/b. For vehicle
maintenance records, see item
number 5.6.003.

retention_statement: Life of Asset
After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year

series_title:
Estimate Files (Supply and
Repair Cost Estimates)
series_description:
Quotes for minor construction and repair
projects and supplies. Includes supporting
documentation, as applicable.

Listing shows all additions, changes,
deletions, and transfer times for the
monthly processing period.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
See item number 5.2.006 for
annual inventory listing.

series_description:
Documentation and reporting on
adherence to procedures that ensure
quality of a process, product, or service.
comments:
See item number 2.2.013 for
quality control records related to IT
procedures.

series_description:
Agency copy of forms completed by
mechanical service personnel for
installation or repair. Includes billing code,
service, labor, parts, and remarks.

Equipment and material descriptions and
specifications that may include but are not
limited to detailed descriptions; lists of
raw materials and ingredients; physical
characteristics of items; special handling
procedures; or technical drawings.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
Reports summarizing efficiency of facility
space utilization, which may include data
on room usage, demand, allocation, and
capacity.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable

series_title:
Building Construction
Contract and Inspection
Records
series_description:
Building construction contracts, surety
bonds, and inspection records.
retention_statement: Life of Asset plus 10 years
comments:
SEE ALSO item numbers 5.2.002
and 5.2.003.

series_description:
Freight or cargo claims against carrier for
damage or loss to a shipment.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
Shipping information, including order
acknowledgements, packing slips, and
related documentation.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable
comments:
CAUTION: Some shipping
information may require longer
retention period. State agencies
must determine if longer retention
period is required based on the
type of materials shipped to or
from their agency.

series_description:
Includes bid requisition/authorizations,
invitations to bid or propose, bid
specifications, accepted and rejected bids,
and bid tabulation/evaluations.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years

series_description:
Invalid bids that do not meet agency
submission requirements and are not
included in bid evaluation process (e.g.
withdrawn, missed submission deadline,
incomplete submission, etc.)
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
Log, register, etc., providing a record of
purchase orders issued, orders received,
and similar data on procurement status.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Requests for information preliminary to
the procurement of goods or services by
direct purchase or bid.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
See agency item number 08.002 for
public information requests.
CAUTION: If the request for
information leads to request for
proposal or bid, the request for
information documentation should
be retained in accordance with
item number 5.3.007.

series_description:
Fire, safety, and other inspection records
of agency facilities and equipment,
including orders issued by inspectors to
correct deficiencies in compliance with
any code or regulations.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include
inspection reports of building
construction. SEE item number
5.2.028.

series_description:
Records of training given to employees in
an agency hazard communications
program. May include but is not limited to
date of class, roster of attendees, subjects
covered, and instructors.
comments:
See item number 3.1.027 for
individual employee training
records.

series_description:
Plan that provides information to
employees about the hazardous chemicals
to which they may be exposed to in their
workplace.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 5 years

series_description:
List of each hazardous chemical normally
present in the workplace.

Safety Data Sheets (SDS) that list
information relating to occupational
safety and health for the use of various
substances and products.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
Logs, registers, or similar records
documenting visitors to limited access or
restricted areas of agency facilities.

Records relating to the issuance of keys,
identification cards, building passes,
passwords, signed statements, or similar
instruments of access to agency or state
facilities, equipment, or automated
systems.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
Plans developed (per Texas Labor Code,
412.054) to mitigate the damage of
potential events that could endanger an
agency’s ability to function. Includes
disaster preparedness, response, and
recovery plans.
retention_statement: Until Superseded
comments:
See item number 5.4.017 for
records related to responding or
recovering from an emergency.

series_description:
Telephone logs created for internal
documentation purposes; station activity
reports of internal listings of
incoming/outgoing telephone activity to
individual telephone stations; operator
call transfers; summary detail reports; and
system activity reports of internal listings
of all incoming/ outgoing agency
telephone activity.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable
comments:
See item number 4.1.001 for
telephone bills.

series_description:
Documentation relating to disputed long‐
distance calls, including documents
evidencing repayment by employees for
personal long distance use.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
Any type of usage report or log used to
monitor utilities such as gas, electric,
water, etc.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include utility
usage reports for agencies that
operate their own utilities.

retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 8 years

series_description:
Relatively long‐lived computer files
containing organized and consistent sets
of complete and accurate electronic
records and associated processing files.
Examples include, but are not limited to,
data tables of relational databases used
by applications or computer programs.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Records management
officers must be certain before
assigning the retention period of
AC to agency automated
processing files that the files do not
fall under records series listed
elsewhere.

series_description:
Agency‐developed automated software
applications, operating system files, and
associated processing files, including job
control language, programs, applications,
scripts, source code, etc.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Software needed for
access to electronic records must
be retained for the period of time
required to access the records.

series_description:
Records documenting operational and
maintenance requirements of computer
hardware such as operating manuals,
hardware/operating system requirements,
hardware configurations, equipment
control systems, and associated
processing files.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Software needed for
access to electronic records must
be retained for the period of time
required to access the records.

series_description:
Records adequate to specify all technical
characteristics necessary for reading or
processing electronic records; to
document modifications to computer
programs; to access, retrieve, manipulate,
and interpret data in an automated
system; and allow for their timely,
authorized disposition. Records include
but are not limited to: user guides, system
or sub‐system definitions, system
specifications, input and output
specifications, system flow charts,
program flow charts, program
maintenance logs, change notices, data
element dictionaries, file layouts, code
books or tables, and other records that
explain the meaning, purpose, structure,
logical relationships, and origin of the data
elements.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Software needed for
access to electronic records must
be retained for the period of time
required to access the records.

series_description:
Files needed for electronic data audits.
Records include but are not limited to:
reports showing transactions accepted,
rejected, suspended, and/or processed;
history files or tapes; records of updates
to application files; and security logs.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
Hardware and software components for
monitoring agency’s computer system
resources and performance.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable
comments:
The disposal of monitoring records
that are automatically overwritten
need not be documented through
destruction authorizations (item
number 1.2.001) or in records
disposition logs (item number
1.2.010), but agencies should
establish procedures governing
disposal of these records as part of
its records management plan (item
number 1.2.014).

series_description:
Manuals, guidelines, or similar documents
establishing data processing policies and
procedures in an agency in such areas as
access and security, systems
development, data retention and
disposition, data ownership, production
control, system back‐up, etc.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 3 years
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include
technical documentation of
procedures necessary for reading
or processing of electronic records.
SEE item number 2.1.009.

series_description:
Forms and logs used to enter and
reconcile data sets submitted for
processing.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
Reports showing transactions that were
accepted, rejected, suspended, and/or
processed.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable

series_description:
Information verifying the quality of
system, hardware, or software operations.
Records include but are not limited to:
records of errors or failures and the loss
of data resulting from such failures;
documentation of abnormal termination
and of error‐free processing; checks of
changes put into production; transaction
histories; and other records needed as an
audit trail to evaluate data accuracy.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
For quality control records related
to non‐IT procedures, see item
number 5.2.018.

series_description:
A record of web pages visited during an
internet session, including data files of
user‐specific information created by the
webserver, that allows users to access
previously visited pages more quickly or to
generate a record of usage of a state‐
owned computer.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable
comments:
The disposal of internet history
records need not be documented
through destruction authorizations
(item number 1.2.001) or in
records disposition logs (item
number 1.2.010), but agencies
should establish procedures
governing disposal of these records
as part of its records management
plan (item number 1.2.014).

series_title:
Software Registrations,
Warranties, and License
Agreements
series_description:
Records documenting the registration and
licensing of a software application to
activate the software for legal use by the
end users of a state agency, along with
warranties providing that the software will
perform in accordance with functional
specifications.
retention_statement: Life of Asset plus 3 years

series_description:
Records documenting the request for and
response to help desk tickets received by
divisions or units, such as information
technology.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable

series_description:
Self‐evaluations and plans documenting
compliance with the requirements of the
Americans with Disabilities Act.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years
comments:
SAO policy is to retain longer than
minimum required by TSLAC.

series_description:
Applications, resumes, transcripts, letters
of reference, and similar documents
whose submission by candidates for
vacant positions is required on the
application form, by application
procedures, or in the employment
advertisement.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include ADA
Accommodation Requests. See
item number 3.1.042.

series_description:
Applications, resumes, transcripts, letters
of reference, and similar documents
whose submission by candidates for
vacant positions is required on the
application form, by application
procedures, or in the employment
advertisement.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description:
Notes, memoranda, or reports relating to
the counseling of an employee for work‐
related, personal, or substance abuse
problems. Usually maintained at the
supervisorial level except in those
agencies with counseling staff.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years
comments:
SAO policy is to retain longer than
minimum required by TSLAC.

series_description:
Agency copies of information relating to
the selection by employees of life,
disability, health, and other types of
insurance offered by the State of Texas to
its employees.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Documents that serve as
payroll deduction authorizations
must be maintained for the
retention period prescribed for
item number 3.2.001.

series_description:
Internal or external announcements or
advertisements of job openings,
promotions, training programs, or
opportunities for overtime.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years
comments:
SAO policy is to retain longer than
minimum required by TSLAC.

retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years
comments:
Includes case studies done during
interviews. CAUTION: Does not
include criminal history checks. SEE
item number 3.1.026. SAO policy is
to retain longer than minimum
required by TSLAC.

series_description:
Records relating to the review of
employee grievances or complaints
against personnel policies, working
conditions, etc.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include formal
complaints filed by an agency
employee with the Equal
Employment Office (EEO) of the
U.S. Department of Labor. SEE item
number 1.1.048. SAO policy is to
retain longer than minimum
required by TSLAC.

series_description: Annual performance evaluations.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years
comments:
SAO policy is to retain longer than
minimum required by TSLAC.

series_description:
Corrective actions are those actions that
do not affect pay, status, or tenure and
are imposed to correct or improve an
employee's job performance.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years
comments:
CAUTION: If, during the retention
period of this records series, any
part of this series is used to
document and support personnel
disciplinary action under item
number 3.1.021, all documentation
from this series used to support
disciplinary action must be retained
for the minimum retention period
described by item number 3.1.021.
SAO policy is to retain longer than
minimum required by TSLAC.

series_description:
Disciplinary actions are those actions that
may affect pay, status, or tenure. They are
imposed to discipline an employee whose
conduct is harmful to the best interests of
the State, the agency, or the employee
work force, and for failure to improve
performance or conduct following
imposition of corrective action. May
include cause for demotion, suspension,
or dismissal, and the reasons for failure to
give written notice of resignation. May
also be used to document evidence of
employee self‐improvement efforts, as
well as favorable and unfavorable
communication.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description:
Forms or similar records used to create or
change information concerning the
records of an employee including pay
grade, position classification, employee
number, evaluation date, and termination
of employment.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years
comments:
SAO policy is to retain longer than
minimum required by TSLAC.

series_description:
Job descriptions, including all associated
task or skill statements, for positions in an
agency.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years
comments:
SAO policy is to retain longer than
minimum required by TSLAC.

series_description:
Criminal history record information on job
applicants or agency employees obtained
from the Department of Public Safety
(DPS).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: An agency that is
authorized to obtain a criminal
history record information from
DPS must refer to its agency's
legislation or see Subchapter F,
Chapter 411 of the Texas
Government Code for appropriate
retention and use of this
information.

series_description:
Certificates of completion, certifications,
licenses, transcripts, test scores, or similar
records documenting the training, testing,
certification, licensing, or continuing
education achievements of an employee.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description: Federal reporting form (Form I‐9).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years
comments:
SAO policy is to retain longer than
minimum required by TSLAC.

series_description:
Agency copies of information relating to
the selection of available benefit options
other than insurance.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years
comments:
CAUTION: Documents that serve as
payroll deduction authorizations
must be maintained for the
retention period prescribed for
item number 3.2.001. SAO policy is
to retain longer than minimum
required by TSLAC.

series_description:
Retention period applies if an agency
replies to the sender of a resume that it
will be kept on file should future job
openings occur.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable
comments:
SEE item number 3.1.014 for
resumes, whether solicited or
unsolicited, that are used in any
way in the employment selection
process.

series_description:
Form completed and signed by employee
or official, or former employee or official,
electing to keep home address, home
telephone number, Social Security
number, and family information open or
confidential under the Public Information
Act, Texas Government Code, Section
552.024.
retention_statement: Until Superseded
comments:
CAUTION: Most recent public
access election information must
be kept as long as the former
employee verification records. SEE
item number 3.3.011.

series_description:
Records and documentation relating to
unemployment compensation claims,
including reimbursement of funds
disbursed by Texas Workforce
Commission (TWC).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description:
Records documenting the amount of
pension or deferred compensation earned
by individual employees.

Reports providing statistical information
on labor force.
comments:
Data pulled from the statewide
system and confirmed by Human
Resources.

series_description:
Minimum information needed to verify
employment, includes name, Social
Security number, exact dates of
employment, last known address, and
most recent public access option form.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 75 years
comments:
Kept in internal HRIS (human
resources information system). See
item number 3.1.038.

series_description:
Records relating to review and monitoring
of job classifications within an agency.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 3 years

series_description:
Any internally distributed manuals,
guidelines, or similar records that define
agencywide policies and procedures
concerning the personnel of an agency.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 3 years

series_description:
Any reports compiled by an agency on
aspects of personnel staffing, including
listings of all staff by program or name,
staff hired during a month, detailed
listings of employees within its
organizational structure, position
vacancies, analyses of turnover rates, and
seasonality of employment, etc.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 3 years

series_description:
Includes documentation used to complete
EEO reports.
comments:
From external HRIS. SAO policy is
to retain longer than minimum
required by TSLAC.

series_description:
Reports, studies, aggregated or summary
data, and similar documentation compiled
to monitor and demonstrate compliance
with the federal Equal Pay Act.
comments:
SAO policy is to retain longer than
minimum required by TSLAC.

series_description:
Records documenting requests for
payment of a sum of money according to
the terms of a policy or contract.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years

series_title:
Occupational Accident
Reports and Associated
Documentation
series_description:
Accident or occupational disease reports
(by supervisors and employees) and other
associated reports required to be
submitted to the State Office of Risk
Management (SORM) or its predecessors
or maintained internally on accident
frequency.
retention_statement: Calendar Year End plus 5 years
comments:
See item number 5.4.014a/b for
non‐employee accidents.

series_description:
Records documenting issuance of parking
permits and assignments.
retention_statement: Until Superseded

series_description:
Employee or applicant requests for
reasonable accommodation under the
ADA (Americans with Disabilities Act).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
From agency legal counsel or the Attorney
General, including any requests eliciting
the opinions.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include legal
opinions or advice rendered on a
matter in litigation or with regard
to pending litigation, or opinions
rendered for Public Information Act
Requests. See item number 1.1.048
and agency item number 08.002.
ARCHIVES NOTE: Opinions and
advice that set legal precedent or
exhibit historical value will be
evaluated by the Archives and
Information Services Division of the
Texas State Library and Archives
Commission for archival
preservation.

series_description:
Includes all correspondence and
documentation relating to requests for
records that are furnished to the public
and those that are exempt under the
Public Information Act (Chapter 552,
Texas Government Code).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
Agency policy to keep this longer
than state requirement. Includes
item numbers 1.1.020 and 1.1.021.

series_description:
Copies of open meetings notices, or any
other documents required by law to be
submitted to the Texas Register.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year

series_description:
Records created by or on behalf of an
agency in anticipation of or in the
adjudication of a lawsuit.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Cases that set
legal precedent or exhibit historical
value will be evaluated by the
Archives and Information Services
Division of TSLAC for archival
preservation.

series_description:
Subpoenas for production of evidence
produced for litigation in which the state
agency is not a party. Includes legal
documents requiring recipient to appear
in court to testify, or to produce records
to be used in litigation.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
For subpoenas related to litigation
in which the state agency is a party,
see item number 1.1.048.

series_description:
Consultation records, notes, letters,
memos, emails, reports, and other
documentation.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include formal
complaint filed with EEOC (Equal
Employment Opportunity
Commission). If matter becomes a
grievance or the subject of
counseling or litigation, or
employee is subject to disciplinary
action, the records are subject to
retention in the appropriate
records series. See item numbers
1.1.048, 3.1.006, 3.1.018, 3.1.020,
and 3.1.021.

series_description: Includes delegated audit reports.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years

series_title:
Risk Assessment and Audit
Plan Records
series_description:
Records used in the creation of the
agency's annual audit plan. Includes final
and draft audit plans and supporting
documentation.

Drafts of proposed legislation and related
correspondence.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable
comments:
Includes cost estimates, fiscal
notes, and impact statements for
the Legislative Budget Board. Also
includes legislative inquiries,
requests for audits and proposed
legislations.

series_description:
Testimony provided to legislative
committees regarding audits or other
agency activities.
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only speeches,
papers, and presentations given by
or on behalf of executive staff,
board or commission members,
division directors and program
heads require archival review.

series_title:
State Employee Exit
Interview Survey
Responses
series_description:
Responses and associated data received
through the Employee Exit Survey System.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 5 years

series_description:
Electronic Classification Analysis System
data.

Full‐Time Equivalent State Employee
System data.
Compensation Analysis Tool System data.

Agency classification information used in
classification compliance audits in
accordance with Texas Government Code,
Chapter 654, Position Classification.

Reports received from state agencies and
institutions of higher education showing
leave granted to employees.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
High‐level summaries of leave granted to
employees by state agencies or
institutions of higher education.

series_title:
Training Materials and
Training Administration
Records
series_description:
Instructional materials and related
administrative records associated with
training provided by the SAO.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 5 years
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include
hazardous material training
records. SEE item number 5.4.007.

series_description:
Documents authorizing final disposition of
records under a certified records retention
schedule. Records may also include
destruction authorizations (e.g. form RMD
102) approved by Texas State Library and
Archives Commission.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years
comments:
CAUTION: If destruction
authorizations are maintained as
part of item number 1.2.010
(records disposition logs), then
longer retention period applies.

series_description:
A records retention schedule (i.e. form
SLR 105) that identifies the records that
are created and maintained by an agency,
provides the minimum timeframes the
records must be retained, and includes
instructions for their disposition. This
series may include working files and
documentation of certification and
approval by the Texas State Library and
Archives Commission.
retention_statement: Until Superseded
comments:
Original is retained for 50 years by
the State and Local Records
Management Division, Texas State
Library and Archives Commission.

series_description:
Forms used to track the transmittal of
records to/from onsite and offsite storage
areas or a transfer of physical custody.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable

series_description:
Logs or similar records listing records
disposed of by an agency, which might
include records series title, dates of
records, and date of disposition.
comments:
CAUTION: Disposition can mean
destroyed or transferred.

series_description:
Agency copy of form RMD 106 granting a
state agency the authorization to store
records at the State Records Center.
retention_statement: Until Superseded
comments: Obsolete record.

series_description:
Worksheets or working papers used to
capture records inventory information,
including location tracking and to
document existence and requirements of
a records series.
retention_statement: Until Superseded

series_description:
Documentation used to provide and
improve access to records. Includes
indexes, card files, shelf lists, registers,
guides, and other finding aids.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: These records must
carry the same retention period
and archival code of the records
they support.

series_description:
Records management plans and similar
records that establish the policies and
procedures under which records and
information are managed in an agency.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 1 year

series_description:
Transmittals and related service
documentation (e.g. form RMD 109) for
disaster recovery services provided by
TSLAC or other entities.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable

series_description:
Forms used by TSLAC (e.g. form RMD 113)
or other entities to establish disaster
recovery services, authorize agency staff
to access the media, etc.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
Records documenting the circulation of
library or file room materials to individual
borrowers.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable

series_description:
Reports submitted to the agency as
required by statute or agreement from
other state agencies or other
organizations other than those listed
elsewhere in this schedule.
